meta.note: Programs, fees, eligibility, and visa policies change frequently. Verify directly with the institution before applying or paying any fee. Self-reported broker match rates are not independently audited.

opportunities.name: International Physician Observership Program
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: Currently not accepting applicants from Belarus, Cuba, Iran, Libya, North Korea, Russia, Syria, or Venezuela
opportunities.specialty: Internal Medicine subspecialties, Pediatrics subspecialties, Otolaryngology, Plastic Surgery, others
requirements: Fully trained physician practicing abroad, OR foreign-program resident/fellow, OR final-year foreign medical student
Not currently residing in the U.S.
Federal regulations prohibit patient care or volunteer research during observership
opportunities.description: Formal 1-month observership granting access to rounds, procedures, and the medical library. Strictly observational.
opportunities.notes: Official program page states the program is not designed for IMGs intending to apply for U.S. residency or fellowship. Many IMGs apply regardless — weigh ethical and eligibility considerations.
warnings: Excludes applicants intending to apply for U.S. residency/fellowship

opportunities.name: Cleveland Clinic Florida Physician Observer Program
opportunities.description: Florida-based Latin American Physician observer opportunities — separate from the main Cleveland Clinic observership and not subject to the same explicit residency-applicant exclusion.
opportunities.notes: Confirm current eligibility for non-Latin American applicants before applying.

opportunities.name: International Observership Program
opportunities.duration: Up to 30 days; start dates every other Monday
opportunities.cost: Free (must secure host MSK faculty sponsor first)
opportunities.applicationWindow: ~6 months in advance via host faculty sponsor
requirements: Fully trained international physician practicing abroad, OR 3rd–4th year international resident/fellow
Recent medical graduates and PGY-1 / PGY-2 residents are NOT eligible
Applicant must independently identify and secure a host MSK faculty sponsor
opportunities.description: Short shadowing in oncology with an MSK faculty host. No fee, but the access path is faculty-sponsored — you must identify and email a host attending who will submit your application.
opportunities.notes: Identify host on PubMed or MSK faculty pages and email with a focused proposal. Limited slots.
warnings: Not designed for IMGs intending to apply for U.S. residency / fellowship
Recent medical graduates and PGY-1/2 ineligible

opportunities.name: Johns Hopkins Bayview Hospital Medicine Observership
requirements: ECFMG certification expected (may be waived under special circumstances)
Mentorship, didactics, mock interviews, and publication opportunities embedded in program
opportunities.description: Structured 6-week observership with didactics, mentorship, mock residency interviews, and explicit publication-opportunity component. One of the more residency-prep-oriented observerships at a top academic center.
opportunities.notes: Premium-priced but with substantially more programmatic support than a typical observership. ECFMG waiver inquiries can go to Dr. Amy Yue Yu.

opportunities.name: Observer Program (Volunteer Services)
requirements: Must independently secure a sponsoring MD inside the institution
No patient contact or EMR access
opportunities.description: Hours-based shadowing administered through Volunteer Services. Useful for low-intensity, low-cost exposure if you can secure a sponsoring physician.
opportunities.notes: Bayview campus has a separate Volunteer Office. Apply through the Volunteer Services portal.

opportunities.name: International Observership Program (Pediatrics)
requirements: Trained physicians or final-year medical students abroad
Application fee non-refundable; tuition via Flywire
opportunities.description: Pediatric subspecialty observerships at one of the largest U.S. children's hospitals. Observation only — no patient contact.
opportunities.notes: Run by the Center for Global Health.

opportunities.name: Clinical Observership Program
requirements: Transcripts, CV, USMLE, ECFMG status, LOR, personal statement, valid U.S. visa
opportunities.description: Inpatient-focused observation in ER, wards, and clinics. Apply via the hospital's GME portal.
opportunities.notes: Recent-graduate focus — older graduates may not qualify.

opportunities.name: International Physician Observerships
opportunities.applicationWindow: After host-faculty acceptance, apply via International Education
requirements: Secure host faculty member / department acceptance first
opportunities.description: Departmental shadowing under a host faculty member. Robust subspecialty coverage at a top-tier academic center.
opportunities.notes: UCLA Health states the observership has no bearing on residency applications and does not provide LORs. UCLA also provides no visa assistance.
warnings: Stated: no bearing on residency applications

opportunities.name: International Observership Program
requirements: IMG or medical graduate (not current student)
Open to IMGs regardless of immigration status
Some departments (e.g., Pediatrics, OB/GYN) periodically excluded
opportunities.description: Structured 4-week observation at an academic medical center. Limited departmental availability — confirm specialty before applying.
opportunities.notes: Open to IMGs regardless of immigration status, which differentiates it from observerships that require non-US-resident status.

opportunities.name: Physician Refresher / Re-entry Program
opportunities.description: Specifically designed for IMGs transitioning to U.S. GME — combines observership / preceptorship with acculturation modules. Graduates report receiving LORs for ERAS.
opportunities.notes: No visa sponsorship or assistance from Drexel.

opportunities.name: Global Observership / William J. Harrington Program
opportunities.description: Established global observership program that has hosted 850+ international participants since 2008. Observe departmental activity for 1–3 months.
opportunities.notes: Distinct from the J-1-sponsored Harrington categorical Internal Medicine residency targeted at Latin American IMGs. For Jackson Health System inquiries: jhs-observership-program@jhsmiami.org.

opportunities.name: International Visiting Medical Observership
opportunities.description: Structured 4- or 8-week shadowing rotations at a Southeastern academic medical center.

opportunities.name: Observer Programs
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: International medical students may apply for observer appointment
requirements: May only shadow one physician / faculty / educator at a time
Certificates and LORs cannot be issued by the Observer program
opportunities.description: Multiple departments offer observer appointments at one of the largest dedicated cancer centers. Strictly shadowing one supervisor at a time.
opportunities.notes: Per MD Anderson policy: certificates and LORs cannot be issued by the Observer program — value of this experience for residency applications is limited unless paired with separate research/volunteer arrangement.
warnings: No certificates or LORs issued by this Observer program

opportunities.name: Clinical Observership Program
opportunities.institution: American Association of Physicians of Indian Origin (AAPI)
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: Open to physicians regardless of national origin (not restricted to Indian-origin)
opportunities.description: Organized network of AAPI-physician hosts offering 4-week observership tutorials with formal contracts. Useful when you lack institutional contacts in your target city.
opportunities.notes: AAPI pays the hosting physician/institution $1,000/month and retains $500/month for administration.

opportunities.name: Departmental Observerships
opportunities.institution: Cook County Health (John H. Stroger Jr. Hospital)
opportunities.description: Safety-net hospital with reports of free observership access in select departments (Trauma, Anesthesiology, Burn). Faculty-sponsored rather than centralized.
opportunities.notes: Persistence and timing (slower summer months) help. No central observership office — contact each department directly.

opportunities.name: AMOpportunities (USCE Aggregator)
requirements: Provides visa invitation letter once reserved
opportunities.description: Largest U.S. clinical experience aggregator — 280+ observerships, clerkships, externships, and virtual rotations across the U.S. Provides B-1/B-2 visa application support and standardized booking.
opportunities.notes: Broker / aggregator, not a primary host. AMO publishes a self-reported 80.6% Match acceptance rate among IMGs who completed AMO rotations and applied (not independently audited). Vet individual rotations carefully — quality varies by underlying host site.
warnings: Third-party broker — verify the host site is a teaching hospital with residency program before paying

opportunities.name: Clinical Rotations & Mentorship Program
opportunities.description: IMG community organization coordinating rotations, mentorship, an annual IMG conference, and an ambassador/chapter program. Founded by Victor Sebastian Arrurana, MD.
opportunities.notes: Community-organization model rather than pure broker — combines rotations with mentorship and conference programming.

opportunities.name: Visiting Medical Student Clerkship
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: Must be in final year of medical school at clerkship time
requirements: Final-year medical student status at the time of clerkship
International applicants must have passed USMLE Step 1
opportunities.description: Hands-on 4-week visiting student rotations at three Mayo campuses (~600 visiting students per year). One of the most respected hands-on rotations available to international medical students.
opportunities.notes: Florida campus: flacomvisit@mayo.edu. Arizona campus: arzclerkship@mayo.edu. Applies only to currently enrolled medical students — not post-graduate IMGs.
warnings: Final-year medical student status required — not for post-graduate IMGs

opportunities.name: IMG Externship
opportunities.description: Private-clinic hands-on externship: supervised history-taking, physical exams, EMR exposure. Includes certificate and letter of recommendation.
opportunities.notes: Verify hands-on activities are consistent with your visa status before participating. Hands-on patient care on B-1/B-2 carries legal risk in some states.

opportunities.name: 4-week Hands-on Externship
opportunities.description: Hands-on primary care externship including patient intake, well-child visits, and chronic care follow-up under supervision. Housing and English training provided.
opportunities.notes: Hands-on patient care on a B-1/B-2 visa is legally constrained — verify visa-activity alignment with an immigration attorney before participating.

opportunities.name: Externship Program
requirements: Application via Somerset Family Medicine intake
opportunities.description: Long-standing AAFP-affiliated externship-style program in New Jersey, popular among unmatched IMGs. Produces LORs on hospital letterhead and a completion certificate.
opportunities.notes: Affiliated with multiple brokers (FMG Portal, AMOpportunities, USMLE Sarthi). Vet through past-client references before paying.

opportunities.name: Pre-Residency Fellowship in Neurocritical Care / Critical Care
opportunities.institution: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: Visa-requiring applicants must already be in U.S. on non-visitor visa
requirements: Visa-requiring: ECFMG certified, USMLE Steps 1, 2, and 3
U.S. citizens / LPR: USMLE Steps 1 and 2 sufficient
Eligible for NY State Limited Permit
opportunities.description: Immersive ICU experience (NSICU, MICU, SICU) with active case management, advanced bedside procedures (arterial lines, central lines, bronchoscopy), ventilator management, and hemodynamic monitoring. High-yield for neurology / critical care / IM residency applications.
opportunities.notes: Director: Dr. John W. Liang. Mount Sinai only sponsors H-1B for individuals already in the U.S. on a non-visitor visa — this is the binding constraint for most international applicants.
warnings: H-1B sponsorship only for applicants already in U.S. on non-visitor visa

opportunities.name: Bridge to Residency Program for Physicians
opportunities.institution: UC San Diego Health Sciences International
opportunities.specialty: Multiple (12-month scholars can rotate in two specialties)
opportunities.description: Certificate-based 6- or 12-month structured bridge program with USMLE prep, acculturation, GME application preparation, clinical skills enhancement, research, and faculty mentorship. Designed as a turnkey IMG pathway.
opportunities.notes: 12-month scholars rotate in two specialties.

opportunities.name: International Medical Graduate (IMG) Program
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: U.S. citizens / Lawful Permanent Residents only
opportunities.applicationWindow: Annual cycle; verify on UCLA site
requirements: Commit to underserved CA service after residency
opportunities.description: Multi-phase bridge program with test prep, supervised clinics, and stipend support. High match rate into California Family Medicine residencies serving underserved Latino communities.
opportunities.notes: Strict citizenship requirement — non-citizens are ineligible regardless of qualifications.
warnings: U.S. citizens / Lawful Permanent Residents only

opportunities.name: Sea Mar IMG Clinical Experience Program
requirements: Receive WA training-license nomination upon acceptance
opportunities.description: Outpatient primary-care immersion (~6 half-day clinics per week + scholarly activities) under family-physician supervision. Pathway includes a WA state training-license nomination.
opportunities.notes: Confirm exact salary/stipend structure and visa sponsorship with the program before committing.

opportunities.name: BRIIDGE Program (Bridge to Residency for Immigrant International Doctor Graduates)
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: U.S. citizens / Lawful Permanent Residents only (immigrant IMGs); not for J-1 applicants
requirements: U.S. citizen or Lawful Permanent Resident
ECFMG certified, USMLE Steps 1 & 2 within 3 attempts
Must not have entered U.S. on J-1 following acceptance into a U.S. program
opportunities.description: 9-month bridge program with inpatient/outpatient training, community and classroom components, preparing immigrant IMGs for U.S. residency.
opportunities.notes: Citizenship and MN residency requirements rule out the program for most non-U.S. IMGs.

opportunities.name: PreResidency Fellowship
opportunities.cost: $1,000/month (covers dormitory bed and meals during externship phase)
opportunities.description: Multi-year fellowship combining internal medicine and cardiovascular medicine training. Modest stipend; private-practice-affiliated rather than ACGME-accredited.
opportunities.notes: Does not sponsor J-1 or H-1B at this time. Multi-year commitment in a private-practice setting carries different residency-application signal than an academic-medical-center fellowship — vet via past participants.

opportunities.name: Medical Research Fellowship Program
opportunities.institution: George Washington University, Office of International Medicine Programs
opportunities.cost: Verify stipend / fee structure with program
opportunities.specialty: Multiple — paired with faculty mentor in specialty of interest
opportunities.description: IMG-targeted research fellowship pairing fellows with GW faculty mentors. Includes grand rounds, conferences, evidence-based medicine training, mock interviews, and CV/personal-statement review.
opportunities.notes: GW reports an 86% historical residency match rate among research fellows (self-reported, not independently audited).

opportunities.name: International Research Fellowship in Trauma, Emergency Surgery, and Surgical Critical Care
opportunities.institution: Massachusetts General Hospital / Harvard Medical School
opportunities.cost: First-year divisional funding very limited; external funding strongly advised
requirements: Dual appointment at MGH and HMS
opportunities.description: Two-year minimum research fellowship splitting clinical data analysis (retrospective, prospective, RCTs) and basic-science work at the Trauma Research Laboratory. Designed for prolific publication output.
opportunities.notes: Chairs: Dr. George Velmahos (Division Chief), Dr. John Hwabejire (Director of Trauma Research). Candidates are strongly advised to secure external funding before applying.

opportunities.name: Gap Year Clinical Research Training Program
opportunities.institution: Mayo Clinic, Division of Orthopedic Surgery Research
opportunities.description: Paid 12-month gap-year clinical research training in orthopedic surgery — historically open to medical students and, with appropriate degree/visa status, IMGs.
opportunities.notes: Confirm IMG eligibility and visa sponsorship in current cycle.

opportunities.name: Postbaccalaureate IRTA Fellowship
opportunities.institution: National Institutes of Health (Intramural Research Program)
opportunities.city: Bethesda / Frederick / Detroit / RTP / Phoenix / Hamilton
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: U.S. citizens and Lawful Permanent Residents only
requirements: Bachelor's within 3 years OR Master's within 6 months of start
Intent to apply to graduate or medical school
opportunities.description: 1–2 year stipended research fellowship at any of 27 NIH Institutes/Centers. Strong springboard for U.S. graduate / medical school applications.
opportunities.notes: Restricted to U.S. citizens and LPR — non-citizen IMGs should pursue the Visiting Fellow Program instead.

opportunities.name: Visiting Fellow Program (NIH Intramural Research Program)
opportunities.description: World-class intramural research training at NIH labs across Bethesda and other campuses. J-1 sponsorship via the NIH Division of International Services.
opportunities.notes: Identify PIs via the NIH RePORTER database and the Office of Intramural Training & Education (OITE) — cold-email a short list of 12–20 aligned PIs.

opportunities.name: Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC) Positions
opportunities.institution: Academic medical centers and clinical trial sites
opportunities.citizenshipRestriction: Requires U.S. work authorization (citizen, LPR, EAD); H-1B sponsorship at this level is rare
opportunities.applicationWindow: Rolling — institutional HR portals + Indeed / LinkedIn
requirements: Some employers prefer prior research / trials experience
opportunities.description: Paid clinical research coordinator roles bridge income with USCE-proximate experience: patient screening, EMR documentation, PI-supervised trial coordination, and frequent co-authorship opportunities. Salaries support family relocation during the application cycle.
opportunities.notes: Some employers (e.g., Revival Research Institute in MI/IL) explicitly recruit IMGs with MBBS/MD into IMG-CRC roles. Saturated market — IMGs compete with domestic candidates with dedicated CRC/public-health degrees.
warnings: Most CRC roles require existing U.S. work authorization

opportunities.name: Postdoctoral Associate / Fellow Positions
opportunities.institution: Yale University, Office for Postdoctoral Affairs
opportunities.description: PI-funded postdoctoral positions across the medical school and affiliated hospitals. J-1 sponsorship managed by Yale Office of International Students & Scholars (OISS).
opportunities.notes: Highly variable by lab — cold-email PIs whose work aligns with your specialty interest.

opportunities.name: Postdoc Innovator Award
opportunities.institution: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
opportunities.specialty: Friedman Brain Institute and other Mount Sinai research institutes
requirements: Existing postdoc affiliation at Mount Sinai
Award application via Office of Postdoctoral Affairs
opportunities.description: Award supplementing postdoc salaries for innovative project proposals.
opportunities.notes: Targeted at postdocs already in residence — use as a layered enhancement after securing a Mount Sinai PI sponsor.

opportunities.name: Research Volunteer Opportunities
requirements: Tailored CV and cover letter to specific PI / lab
opportunities.description: Volunteer research roles in oncology — usually obtained by cold-emailing labs aligned with your interest. Strong publication potential and access to a deep faculty network.
opportunities.notes: Apply via the institution's volunteer portal AND direct PI emails — the two paths are complementary.

opportunities.name: Volunteer Research Positions
requirements: Onboarding through HR and research administration
opportunities.description: Volunteer research positions at one of the largest dedicated cancer centers. Distinct from the Observer program — research engagement can produce co-authorship and LORs.
opportunities.notes: Texas is a top-10 IMG match state — pairing MD Anderson research with Houston/Texas program networking is a common gap-year strategy.

opportunities.name: Research Volunteer Opportunities
opportunities.description: A Harvard-affiliated AMC with a deep research bench across specialties. Volunteer roles are typically lab-specific and obtained by cold-emailing PIs.
opportunities.notes: Cross-reference PubMed for active investigators in your specialty before emailing.

opportunities.name: Volunteer Research (Lerner Research Institute)
opportunities.description: Volunteer roles at the Lerner Research Institute — basic, clinical, and translational science across departments. Distinct from the observership program (which excludes residency applicants).
opportunities.notes: Unlike the Cleveland Clinic observership program, research volunteering does not carry the same exclusion of residency-applicant IMGs — but verify in writing with the specific lab.

opportunities.name: Research Volunteer Opportunities
opportunities.description: Volunteer research roles across Mayo's three campuses. Distinct from the Visiting Medical Student Clerkship (which is hands-on but requires final-year student status).
opportunities.notes: Mayo has historically been receptive to IMG volunteer researchers with strong CVs and clear research goals.

opportunities.name: Hands-on Clinical Skills Workshops
opportunities.description: Multi-day simulation workshops in advanced suturing, radiology, advanced/difficult airway, and ultrasound. High-yield procedural exposure in a controlled environment.
opportunities.notes: Confirm current scheduled dates and locations on the provider website.

opportunities.name: Low-Resource Healthcare Skills Training
opportunities.description: Specialized training in low-resource healthcare skills — wound care, newborn resuscitation, basic obstetrics. Useful for primary care and global-health-oriented applicants.

opportunities.name: MERIT Academy Online Certifications
opportunities.institution: Association of Pakistani Physicians of North America (APPNA)
opportunities.description: Affordable online certifications developed by U.S. sub-specialty faculty. Low-cost CV additions accessible from anywhere.

opportunities.name: Clinical Research Coordinator Certification
opportunities.institution: Association of Clinical Research Coordination & Compliance (ACRCC)
opportunities.description: Operational, job-ready CRC certification tailored for transitioning healthcare professionals. Signals immediate competence to clinical trial employers when applying to CRC roles.
opportunities.notes: Useful when paired with active CRC job applications.

opportunities.name: ACLS / PALS / BLS Certifications
opportunities.description: Widely recognized U.S. certifications. Quick to obtain and a basic line item programs often expect.
opportunities.notes: Verify your certification is AHA-issued (some programs only accept AHA, not international equivalents).

opportunities.name: Global Clinical Scholars Research Training (GCSRT)
requirements: Clinician or scientist with active research interests
Application via HMS Postgraduate Medical Education
opportunities.description: Blended-learning certificate program for international clinicians and scientists — experimental clinical research, data interpretation, grant writing, and in-person Boston workshops.
opportunities.notes: Certificate (not a degree), so no F-1 visa pathway — but Harvard branding is a strong CV signal.

opportunities.name: ECHO (ECFMG Certificate Holders Office) — Free Career Resources
opportunities.applicationWindow: Available to ECFMG-certified IMGs and applicants approaching certification
opportunities.description: Free Intealth resource (expanded from the former Acculturation Program). Career milestone guidance, advisor-network mentoring, training videos, customizable orientation survival guide.
opportunities.notes: Highest-quality free resource for ECFMG-certified IMGs entering U.S. training. Includes the free Headspace app benefit for J-1 physicians.

opportunities.name: USMLE Step Preparation Courses
opportunities.specialty: USMLE Step 1, Step 2 CK, Step 3 + CIS / SEP / ICE skills
opportunities.description: Live Online, On Demand, Q-banks, and simulated practice exams for the USMLE sequence. In-person hubs in major U.S. cities.
opportunities.notes: Combine with UWorld Q-bank (industry standard for Step 2 CK) and AMBOSS / OnlineMedEd / Lecturio as supplementary platforms.

opportunities.name: Master of Medical Sciences in Clinical Investigation (MMSCI)
requirements: Choose Clinical Investigation or Translational Investigation track
opportunities.description: Two-year Harvard mentored-research degree producing two first-author manuscripts. The flagship clinical-investigation master's for IMGs aiming at research-heavy specialties.
opportunities.notes: F-1 visa pathway with CPT/OPT options.

opportunities.name: Master of Science in Clinical Research (MS-CR)
requirements: Residents, fellows, junior faculty, or MD-equivalent graduates
opportunities.description: Accelerated one-year clinical research master's for clinicians. Capstone is a systematic review or meta-analysis.
opportunities.notes: F-1 visa pathway for international students.

opportunities.name: MPH — Epidemiology and Clinical Research Track
opportunities.description: MPH track specifically aimed at clinicians developing research and biostatistics expertise. Strong epidemiological methods training and faculty mentorship.
opportunities.notes: F-1 visa pathway provides CPT/OPT options for research roles related to your field of study.

opportunities.name: Master of Public Health
opportunities.institution: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
requirements: Significant prior public health, clinical, or research experience
opportunities.description: World-renowned MPH with concentrations across global health, epidemiology, health policy, biostatistics, and more. High brand recognition on a residency application.
opportunities.notes: Tuition and cost-of-living make this best for applicants with funding or a clear research/policy career hedge.

opportunities.name: Master of Public Health
opportunities.institution: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
opportunities.description: MPH with seven specialty tracks, including a combined option with General Surgery residency for those completing surgical training at Sinai.

opportunities.name: MS in Clinical and Translational Research
opportunities.institution: University of Connecticut — Cato T. Laurencin Institute
opportunities.description: Designed for MDs and other terminal-degree holders. Emphasis on research practica: grant application, publishable report, oral defense. Strong fit for IMGs targeting research-heavy specialties.

opportunities.name: Master of Public Health
opportunities.institution: Tulane Celia Scott Weatherhead School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine
opportunities.description: Long-standing public-health program in the South with strong tropical medicine ties. Available standalone or as MD/MPH integrated.

opportunities.name: Master of Public Health
opportunities.institution: University of Minnesota School of Public Health
opportunities.description: Strong public health program with concentrations relevant to primary care, preventive medicine, and global health. F-1 visa pathway with CPT/OPT.

grants.name: PERF Elterman & Shields Research Grants
grants.sponsor: Child Neurology Society (CNS) / Pediatric Epilepsy Research Foundation (PERF)
grants.eligibility: Child neurologists or developmental pediatricians; Early-Stage Investigator (≤10 years from terminal degree)
grants.notes: PERF-CNS Bridge Grant also supports researchers unfunded on first NIH R01.

grants.name: HONORS Award
grants.eligibility: Trainees pursuing hematology research projects (3–12 months)

grants.name: Student Scholarships in Cerebrovascular / Cardiovascular Disease & Stroke
grants.eligibility: Minimum 2-month commitment; spring application cycle (≈April 2 deadline)

grants.name: Fogarty International Clinical Research Scholars (VECD)
grants.eligibility: Mentored clinical research in Haiti, Tanzania, or Brazil

grants.name: ACS International Scholarships
grants.eligibility: Quality improvement focus (ACS/AAST trauma, ACS/ASBrS breast cancer)
grants.notes: ACS Community Surgeons Travel Awards also support Clinical Congress attendance.

grants.name: Family Medicine Leads Scholarships
grants.notes: Resident Service Award and Emerging Leader Institute ($1,000) also available.

grants.name: IDSA ERF / NFID Merle A. Sande / Pfizer Fellowship Award
grants.sponsor: Infectious Diseases Society of America Education & Research Foundation
grants.eligibility: International infectious diseases research; open to IMGs per IDSA listing
grants.notes: PIDS maintains additional IMG-eligible funding lists.

strategyGuides.name: The Unmatched IMG: A 12-Month Plan
strategyGuides.desc: Strategic playbook for IMGs who did not match — diagnostic archetypes (low scores, older YOG, no USCE, interviewed-but-didn't-match), gap-year remediation, and the Application 2.0 narrative.

strategyGuides.name: The IMG's Strategic Bridge to Residency: Paid Research
strategyGuides.desc: Deep-dive guide to paid research as a residency bridge — Build / Buy / Trap framework, visa pathways, PI-centric vs. posted-position search, and analysis of premier institutions.

strategyGuides.name: Funding U.S. Graduate Education for IMGs
strategyGuides.desc: Strategic guide to MPH / MBA scholarships, tiered funding sources, citizenship vs. ethnicity barriers, graduate assistantships, and a prioritized action plan.

directories.name: USCEHub
directories.desc: Directory of 200+ U.S. clinical experience programs (observerships, externships, research) with filters for state, specialty, fees, and IMG eligibility.

directories.name: AAMC Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) & Extramural Electives Compendium (EEC)
directories.desc: AAMC-managed platforms for away rotations and electives at LCME-accredited medical schools. Primarily for current medical students.

directories.name: AMA International Medical Education
directories.desc: Top-level AMA pages on USCE, observerships, visa requirements, and the IMG Section toolkit.

directories.name: mymedschool.org observership guide
directories.desc: Periodically updated curated lists of IMG-friendly hospitals offering observerships.

directories.name: AMOpportunities
directories.desc: Largest U.S. clinical experience aggregator (broker). 280+ observerships, clerkships, externships, and virtual rotations. Visa invitation letter included. Vet underlying hosts before paying.

residency.name: FREIDA (AMA Residency & Fellowship Database)
residency.desc: Search 13,000+ ACGME-accredited programs with filters for IMG metrics, visa support, and specialty-specific requirements. Free tier + paid AMA membership tiers.

residency.name: Residency Explorer
residency.desc: Free tool integrating verified AAMC, NRMP, and ACGME data — interview invitation data, resident demographics, USMLE expectations.

residency.name: Match A Resident
residency.desc: Aggregates program-level visa policies, USCE expectations, and ECFMG certification deadlines into customized compatibility scores. Paid service.

residency.name: NRMP Match Data
residency.desc: Official NRMP main residency match statistics — IMG match rates by specialty and state. 2025 cycle: 60.3% Active Applicant Placement Rate for non-U.S. citizen IMGs.

official.name: ECFMG / Intealth
official.desc: Authoritative on ECFMG certification, the Pathways 1–6 system, USMLE pathway for IMGs, and sole sponsor of J-1 physicians in U.S. clinical training.

official.name: ECHO (ECFMG Certificate Holders Office)
official.desc: Free Intealth career-planning resources for certified IMGs — milestone guidance, advisor-network mentoring, training videos.

official.name: U.S. State Department — J-1 Physician Program (BridgeUSA)
official.desc: Official guidance on the J-1 Alien Physician category for U.S. graduate medical education.

official.name: NIH Office of Intramural Training & Education (OITE)
official.desc: NIH-wide training program portal — Visiting Fellow pathway for non-U.S. citizens, mentor identification, application guidance.

official.name: NIH RePORTER
official.desc: Database of NIH-funded research projects. Use to find Principal Investigators whose work aligns with your interests before cold-emailing.

networking.name: Doximity
networking.desc: Physician network with residency rankings and faculty directories. Useful for cross-referencing publication output and identifying potential mentors.

networking.name: ResearchGate
networking.desc: Researcher network with publication histories — combine with Doximity to surface active investigators in your target specialty.

networking.name: LinkedIn (IMG-focused groups)
networking.desc: IMG advocacy organizations and individual physicians frequently post free/low-cost USCE leads and answer outreach DMs.

networking.name: Foundation of IMG
networking.desc: IMG advocacy and mentorship organization — volunteer opportunities, school ambassador roles, peer mentorship.

networking.name: Project IMG
networking.desc: IMG community organization coordinating rotations, mentorship, an annual IMG conference, and an ambassador/chapter program.
